title: Linear Models, Regression, and Systems · Quantity Clinic: System Solutions
summary: Allocate staff and supplies under multiple linear constraints. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int system solutions increment in a linear allocation and scenario model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Solve linear systems and classify unique, absent, and infinite solutions.
Fit a linear regression and inspect residuals before using its predictions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate System Solutions evidence
steps.instruction: A model passes through (3, 11) and (7, 23). Calculate its average rate of change. Use the result to decide System Solutions for a linear allocation and scenario model.
steps.why: The System Solutions result for a linear allocation and scenario model is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (23 − 11) / (7 − 3). Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Solve linear systems and classify unique, absent, and infinite solutions.
